questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: In the context of skill development initiatives in India, how does the Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) scheme primarily differ from the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)?

options.text: JSS focuses on high-end technology sectors like AI and Robotics, while PMKVY focuses on traditional crafts.
JSS is a Central Sector Scheme, whereas PMKVY is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme implemented by states.
JSS specifically targets non-literates and school drop-outs in rural areas, while PMKVY provides short-term skill training to youth.
JSS provides only financial incentives to employers, while PMKVY provides direct training to candidates.

questions.explanation: The Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) scheme is distinct because it targets non-literates, neo-literates, and school drop-outs, particularly in rural regions. PMKVY, on the other hand, is a broader scheme providing free short-duration skill training and certification to the youth to enhance employability.
questions.takeaway: JSS is uniquely positioned to address the skill needs of the non-literate and rural school drop-out population, distinguishing it from general youth-centric skill programs.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS): Provides vocational training to non-literates, neo-literates and school drop-outs in rural regions. Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (2015): Free short-duration skill training and skill certification to youth.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: The 'India Skills Accelerator' initiative, recently seen in the news, is a collaborative effort between the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) and which international organization?
questions.explanation: The India Skills Accelerator was launched by the MSDE in collaboration with the World Economic Forum (WEF). Its goal is to align education with industry needs, specifically in emerging sectors like AI and clean energy.
questions.takeaway: The India Skills Accelerator is a high-profile partnership between the Indian government and the World Economic Forum (WEF).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE), in collaboration with the World Economic Forum (WEF), launched “India Skills Accelerator”.
